name: =?utf-8?Q?Sean=20Rose=2C=20Team=20Janet?=
subject: Do you want to help replace liberal Kendra Horn with a Trump supporter?
body:
Get involved with #TeamJanet today! | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/d79aaffe00d4/calling-all-patriots-12435054?e=795c6d9796)
---
| | [ ](https://janet2020.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a78cce64304e05c22b2864ace&id=278c1ce9fc&e=795c6d9796)
---
| | Peter,

As you may know, the **race for Congress in OK-5 is the NUMBER ONE pickup opportunity** for Republicans in the country.

So we're working hard here at the Janet Barresi campaign to **build a strong team of grassroots supporters** to beat liberal Democrat Kendra Horn.

Simply put, Horn is a big ally of Nancy Pelosi and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez... [we need your help](https://janet2020.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a78cce64304e05c22b2864ace&id=8de1a65812&e=795c6d9796) to **kick Horn out of Congress** and **replace her with a strong Trump ally**!

I'm working night and day to get Janet elected on June 30th and thankful you have chosen to join Janet's winning team. But we need your help.

Would you [consider signing up to volunteer for #TeamJanet](https://janet2020.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a78cce64304e05c22b2864ace&id=2adfda77d7&e=795c6d9796)?

We're looking for folks to do any or all of these action items:
* _**Make phone calls**_ to voters (we'll provide numbers and scripts).
* _**Endorse Janet**_ (lend your name publicly).
* Be a _**local captain**_ (help organize in your area).
* Join the _**online action team**_ (post on [Facebook](https://janet2020.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a78cce64304e05c22b2864ace&id=225e112440&e=795c6d9796), etc)

**To get involved with #TeamJanet, [click here](https://janet2020.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a78cce64304e05c22b2864ace&id=9c4a3c9e01&e=795c6d9796).**

I encourage you to share this message with any friends and family who would be interested in helping Janet become our next congresswoman. A little goes a long way, but we need as much involvement as possible.

Please feel free to forward this email to them with my contact email [Sean@Janet2020.com](https://janet2020.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a78cce64304e05c22b2864ace&id=3985add32e&e=795c6d9796), or [send them to our website](https://janet2020.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a78cce64304e05c22b2864ace&id=d35b765dc1&e=795c6d9796) so they can get signed up.

Thank you for your support for Team Janet and I will be in touch soon with more campaign trail updates!

For Oklahoma,

Sean Rose
Grassroots Engagement & Communications Advisor
Janet Barresi for Congress 2020
www.Janet2020.com

_P.S. Our campaign remains committed to the safety of our community, staff and supporters as we face the COVID-19 situation.[Visit our Coronavirus Resource Center for more information.](https://janet2020.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a78cce64304e05c22b2864ace&id=10e4b20cc7&e=795c6d9796)_
---
| |
---

**Our mailing address is:**
Janet for CongressPO Box 20147Oklahoma City, OK 73156-0147

Want to change how you receive these emails?

name: Tom Steyer
subject: I will match your donation to the COVID-19 Relief Fund
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TOM STEYER 2020]()
body:
| | | |
---
---
Friend:
When I announced last summer that I was going to run for president, it was because far too many people believe that the system has left them behind. There is an underlying injustice in America that’s very clear, and I felt a serious, uncompromising duty to do the right thing and try to address it. Though my campaign has ended — and our world has been thrust into uncertain and difficult times — those things remain deeply true.

**That’s why I’m partnering with BET and United Way right now to match donations to their COVID-19 Relief Fund.** [_**Today is Giving Tuesday Now, a global day of unity to support the organizations that need our help. Will you make a donation as a part of this critical effort?**_](http://clicks.tomsteyer.com/ls/click?upn=2PbTaIH-2Bu1BICxGw6BOoZq-2BljAuiIpYeat0iDKY7eH2goH-2F-2BGtdMcMfyEmRNX1rU3r58g8u1jg2-2Ffk71ixLZBqi1seoII0OsH6kbbFFAcmM-3DSljd_XwU9lPq5-2Bze3gI64ohlV0cQwWAuBAU7ADIvj2CS7ymj-2F7ZKPx3NVKw8kjoD6Gq3c-2BrItP5taLUBrsAr-2FjZetFIJTu2XOSYWw-2BRTsCZVEZUmXdgh-2BenInj6gTOMryUZZw7Nk3sKzCJB55xi-2FZcSsGEgnDWqJtN0wEnXaEBGfw9aoX7ZV3nxmw5KRtM4vS-2F-2F80Szix11AgIuDeC9mxHkx-2FNCvUc-2BSJ3NJ0zqiMTc93bynZFnUPJtbFhyHDVl3T7QMtZt0LPvq4-2FOcfUTMNuXcjdY2A399devx9-2FGHrxdx3XvV-2FdXIbAFm5ll39felAyBeaCQKjE-2Bzj0Yfo7Qqu7rFtWDwL2WOHLmPvSMLfxe71tpcyaOBY6SyCytizXRY8sLLHFyHeekZrMpB9RdQcWiAgEyuicl3uopQuLr4ty6IdeMnmUu-2FJDgxCNtXrqjaZnnxmtHRCL1l2fGbcl7I7uK8lAoGiT8Rx-2FsOjEUIf7-2BqhOxCSnGfNtUn83PLyvRj-2BRNyEpigwrwVj6c7VJKgBzFY01ZFF8uMnjzlriOzFrVcOr7FQNq6t2Ge5jX8I90lCljiZgPVj645RMJPMg-2BUERPuRx-2BDYToSg2QbGqw7eaR1zzo0mH8whO39Z58MZ81O-2FywYHncCQQyHVY1yID-2B1S87Ogc1F1cU37ryGacxaXLdgpuCdUwG46zm6L-2B-2FZ6A9H4EadySDyMI2gve9EP-2FlkYGC0n2FMa2VIyC7hTvVH5LOcMKXEo-2FsfuYhIUlatDDSXW1NpjFnSdyAs5FS1he9ArwJgzdcNYgRA4fUKoyBy8Ag1oh-2BbIO9Ay6OBsmDTO4vKl8D46fxJIov5c3DXZTPZFohXsjA-3D-3D)

This is so important to me. We know that the coronavirus outbreak is a public health emergency, and an economic crisis, but it’s also a racial justice issue. The African American community is being impacted by this pandemic at a completely disproportionate rate. And that’s not a coincidence. It’s due to generations of pervasive prejudice and racial discrimination that has faced African Americans in this country.

I have often said that every single policy area in America has a gigantic subtext of racial justice — or more accurately, injustice. Criminal justice. Housing. Loans. Health care. Pollution. And this virus is no different. **That’s a failure.**

And now I’m asking you to stand with me in doing some small part to address the disproportionate impact of the coronavirus pandemic on the African American community. [_**Today, on Giving Tuesday, I will personally match the first $100,000 this team contributes to the BET-United Way COVID-19 Relief Fund.**_](http://clicks.tomsteyer.com/ls/click?upn=2PbTaIH-2Bu1BICxGw6BOoZq-2BljAuiIpYeat0iDKY7eH2goH-2F-2BGtdMcMfyEmRNX1rU3r58g8u1jg2-2Ffk71ixLZBqi1seoII0OsH6kbbFFAcmM-3DNqjF_XwU9lPq5-2Bze3gI64ohlV0cQwWAuBAU7ADIvj2CS7ymj-2F7ZKPx3NVKw8kjoD6Gq3c-2BrItP5taLUBrsAr-2FjZetFIJTu2XOSYWw-2BRTsCZVEZUmXdgh-2BenInj6gTOMryUZZw7Nk3sKzCJB55xi-2FZcSsGEgnDWqJtN0wEnXaEBGfw9aoX7ZV3nxmw5KRtM4vS-2F-2F80Szix11AgIuDeC9mxHkx-2FNCvUc-2BSJ3NJ0zqiMTc93bynZFnUPJtbFhyHDVl3T7QMtZt0LPvq4-2FOcfUTMNuXcjdY2A399devx9-2FGHrxdx3XvV-2FdXIbAFm5ll39felAyBeaCQKjE-2Bzj0Yfo7Qqu7rFtWDwL2WOHLmPvSMLfxe71tpcyaOBY6SyCytizXRY8sLLHFyHeekZrMpB9RdQcWiAgEyuicl3uopQuLr4ty6IdeMnmUu-2FJDgxCNtXrqjaZnnxmtHRCL1l2fGbcl7I7uK8lAoGiT8Rx-2FsOjEUIf7-2BqhOxCSnGfNtUn83PLyvRj-2BRNyEpigwrwVj6c7VJKgBzFY01ZFF8uMnjzlriOzFrVcOr7FQNq6t2Ge5jX8I90lCljiZgPVj645RMJPMg-2BUERPuRx-2BDYToSg2QbGqw7eaR1zzo0mH8whO39Z58MZ81O-2FywYH8XNaBWGBhIAnAad13AXnvl3y-2FElfufkBJRcReq7gCO-2BqK-2FlbZhj58fMiVlmKtaShs0NUzn10li1lzlNCKw-2FX6OS7ZwxwWiudHHAMua1AdbVNusXisGXqx85sGX3x2rkC2yPer3s1-2FZP-2B06ETsKHOs46LSIMuFin9uW9PlZ51zacpwsK23bauMsJLVCLtJip1XJSQcXwfZ5Qu3nntIWqx5Q-3D-3D)

**We are all connected, in this society and on this planet. So let’s honor that connection by standing together right now. Please donate what you can, and I will proudly match your contribution with my own:**

clean_body: | | | | Friend: When I announced last summer that I was going to run for president, it was because far too many people believe that the system has left them behind. There is an underlying injustice in America that’s very clear, and I felt a serious, uncompromising duty to do the right thing and try to address it. Though my campaign has ended — and our world has been thrust into uncertain and difficult times — those things remain deeply true. That’s why I’m partnering with BET and United Way right now to match donations to their COVID-19 Relief Fund. This is so important to me. We know that the coronavirus outbreak is a public health emergency, and an economic crisis, but it’s also a racial justice issue. The African American community is being impacted by this pandemic at a completely disproportionate rate. And that’s not a coincidence. It’s due to generations of pervasive prejudice and racial discrimination that has faced African Americans in this country. I have often said that every single policy area in America has a gigantic subtext of racial justice — or more accurately, injustice. Criminal justice. Housing. Loans. Health care. Pollution. And this virus is no different. That’s a failure. And now I’m asking you to stand with me in doing some small part to address the disproportionate impact of the coronavirus pandemic on the African American community. We are all connected, in this society and on this planet. So let’s honor that connection by standing together right now. Please donate what you can, and I will proudly match your contribution with my own: | Thank you, Tom Steyer | | | "Facebook" | "Twitter" | "Instagram" ---|---|--- Please visit for information and resources to help slow the spread of COVID-19 and prevent infection. Follow CDC guidelines, including social distancing to avoid large groups and unnecessary contact with others, and adhere to instruction from your local and state elected officials. We want to make sure you're receiving relevant information about how you can help us do what's right. If you’d rather not receive any emails from us, you can unsubscribe by clicking — we'll be sad to see you go! Tom Steyer 2020 · PO Box 626 · San Francisco, CA 94104 · USA [PAID FOR BY TOM STEYER 2020]()

name: Kathy Manning
subject: I'm so grateful
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kathy Manning for Congress
body:
you're on our team
|
---

| | I couldn't do this without you -- I want to thank everyone on this grassroots team who took action last month and for continuing to be engaged with our campaign, especially during a time that I know is challenging for so many. This grassroots team's engagement is essential to the success of our campaign. The failures of the Trump Administration remind us that our mission to flip this critical red seat and defend the House Majority is more important than ever. Your contributions help us build a strong campaign that has the resources to get out the vote for not only NC-06 but for 2020 races up and down the ballot. We see the results of this team's hard work every day. Just last month, we:
* Received the endorsement of Congresswoman Gabby Giffords' gun-safety organization, **Giffords PAC** , as well as endorsements from **NARAL, Planned Parenthood, and the National Organization for Women, and Equality North Carolina** \-- all of which are essential to the fight for equality.

* **Held our very first virtual town hall** , discussing the impact of COVID-19 on mental health. I'm looking forward to more virtual town halls as we find new ways to stay connected.

| [](https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/page/m/18d9d312/3d199a51/2f4afed1/7364fed5/2895793693/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIxNzQyNiIsImUzdHpiSFZuZlgwPSI6ImttZTIwMDUwNSIsImUzdDJaWEp6YVc5dWZYMD0iOiJuYSJ9/?g=Bm8FRTjfd5ld5gouxTH21cA "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kme200505?refcode=kme200505_na_other&refcode2=17426")
---
During such difficult times, your engagement is vital to our efforts to make health care more affordable, improve access to high-quality education, and make the economy work for everyone. I'm so grateful to have you on our team.
Thank you,
Kathy
| | [Donate](https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/page/m/18d9d312/3d199a51/2f4afed1/7364fed5/2895793693/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIxNzQyNiIsImUzdHpiSFZuZlgwPSI6ImttZTIwMDUwNSIsImUzdDJaWEp6YVc5dWZYMD0iOiJuYSJ9/?g=Bm8FRTjfd5ld5gouxTH21cA "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kme200505?refcode=kme200505_na_other&refcode2=17426")

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: President Trump in Arizona today
body:
American manufacturing is at the heart of our battle against the Coronavirus.

https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=80a02e2176&e=3ee4985eea
The White House • May 5, 2020

** WATCH: President Trump visits repurposed factory in Arizona
------------------------------------------------------------
American manufacturing is at the heart of our battle against the Coronavirus. Companies across the country have heeded President Trump’s call and repurposed entire factories to make personal protective equipment and other needed supplies.

Honeywell is one such business with an incredible story to tell. Today, President Trump saw firsthand how the company transformed an aerospace facility in Arizona—one that designed and manufactured aircraft propulsion engines and auxiliary power units—into a nimble face-mask production line.

As a result, Honeywell is expected to produce more than 20 million N95 masks per month. This expanded operation has allowed the firm to begin hiring 500 additional workers at its Phoenix facility, creating jobs during a tough time for many Americans.

🎬 President Trump: “You make America proud!” (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2947b199d0&e=3ee4985eea)

America’s private sector, the best on Earth, is too valuable to leave on the sidelines during this global pandemic. Rather than rely on government alone, President Trump has led the largest mobilization of both public and private sectors since World War II, using every resource available to stop this invisible enemy.

That mobilization includes leveraging the Defense Production Act to secure massive amounts of needed supplies. In one agreement alone, 3M agreed to produce 166.5 million masks in just 3 months. The Administration is also procuring 100,000 ventilators in 100 days—three times more than the country normally makes in a year.

“The people of our country are warriors,” President Trump said.

Watch: Honeywell did a 9-month job in less than 5 weeks! (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d0e6135b2d&e=3ee4985eea)

** Historic support for our Native American communities
------------------------------------------------------------
President Trump’s first stop in Arizona was a roundtable discussion on support for Native Americans during this difficult time. Meeting with local and tribal leaders, the President said that no community will be left behind as we unite against this virus.

🎬 President Trump: We will do everything possible to support Native Americans! (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7deaa8ab3d&e=3ee4985eea)

The CARES Act, signed by President Trump in March, authorizes $8 billion to support tribal governments with response and recovery needs. Today, the Trump Administration announced that it is disbursing the first $4.8 billion batch of those funds, which make for the largest investment in a single program for Indian Country in our history.

President Trump is uniquely committed to supporting the wellbeing of our American Indian and Alaska Native communities. Last year, he signed the first-ever presidential proclamation recognizing the grave issue of missing and murdered Native Americans.

How President Trump is protecting Native Americans as we fight Coronavirus (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=59b59a5c68&e=3ee4985eea)

** Photo of the Day
------------------------------------------------------------
President Trump disembarks Air Force One in Phoenix, Arizona | May 5, 2020

Privacy Policy (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=38c6118239&e=3ee4985eea) | Contact the White House (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f075f8eefe&e=3ee4985eea) | Unsubscribe (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=2f8b90c486)

============================================================
The White House . 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW . Washington, DC 20500 . USA · 202-456-1111

() Historic support for our Native American communities President Trump’s first stop in Arizona was a roundtable discussion on support for Native Americans during this difficult time. Meeting with local and tribal leaders, the President said that no community will be left behind as we unite against this virus. 🎬 President Trump: We will do everything possible to support Native Americans! () The CARES Act, signed by President Trump in March, authorizes $8 billion to support tribal governments with response and recovery needs. Today, the Trump Administration announced that it is disbursing the first $4.8 billion batch of those funds, which make for the largest investment in a single program for Indian Country in our history. President Trump is uniquely committed to supporting the wellbeing of our American Indian and Alaska Native communities. Last year, he signed the first-ever presidential proclamation recognizing the grave issue of missing and murdered Native Americans.
